title: The user and group that the folder belongs to are modified
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

``` sh
$ mkdir foo
$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 2 alice alice 6 Jan 28 13:57 foo
$ podman run -d --name foo --net foo -v $PWD/foo:/consul/data consul agent
d7a72cb88a10fc2fab9cc6c937a4360d36e0c4ee42109858af8e8f9b407436b8
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                                                                      COMMAND         CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
5d1c9e7a2157  quay.io/libpod/rootless-cni-infra@sha256:304742d5d221211df4ec672807a5842ff11e3729c50bc424ea0cea858f69d7b7  sleep infinity  9 seconds ago  Up 7 seconds ago          rootless-cni-infra
d7a72cb88a10  docker.io/library/consul:latest                                                                            agent           9 seconds ago  Up 3 seconds ago          foo
$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 3 10099 10999 33 Jan 28 13:59 foo
$ ls -l foo/
total 8
-rw-r--r-- 1 10099 10999 394 Jan 28 13:59 checkpoint-signature
-rw------- 1 10099 10999  36 Jan 28 13:59 node-id
drwxr-xr-x 2 10099 10999  28 Jan 28 13:59 serf
$ podman stop foo
WARN[0000] cannot toggle freezer: cgroups not configured for container
WARN[0000] lstat : no such file or directory
d7a72cb88a10fc2fab9cc6c937a4360d36e0c4ee42109858af8e8f9b407436b8
$ podman network ls
NAME    VERSION  PLUGINS
foo     0.4.0    bridge,portmap,firewall,tuning,dnsname
$ cat /etc/subuid
alice:10000:65536
$ cat /etc/subgid
alice:10000:65536
```

**Describe the results you received:**

The user of the folder is modified

``` sh
$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 3 10099 10999 33 Jan 28 13:59 foo
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

``` sh
$ ls -l
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 2 alice alice 6 Jan 28 13:57 foo
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   a0d478edea7f775b7ce32f8eb1a01e75374486cb
Built:        Wed Dec  9 05:48:23 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Win10 Hyper-V

I create a generation 2 virtual machine in Hyper-V

``` sh
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Arch Linux"
PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
ID=arch
BUILD_ID=rolling
ANSI_COLOR="38;2;23;147;209"
HOME_URL="https://www.archlinux.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://wiki.archlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://bbs.archlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.archlinux.org/"
LOGO=archlinux
```

comments.body:
I guess that is expected behavior because inside the consol container the process is started as non-root too
https://github.com/hashicorp/docker-consul/blob/master/0.X/Dockerfile#L13
```
/ # id consul
uid=100(consul) gid=1000(consul) groups=1000(consul),1000(consul)
```
This is expected. Read https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-makes-sense for a good explanation.
If you want alice to be the same uid within the container as opposed to root, you can use --userns=keep-id.
